metadata.title: Wave Packet Spreading — Group vs Phase Velocity

beats.narration_text: Olá. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of wave packet spreading. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of wave packet spreading
output: ✓ Wave Packet Spreading — Group vs Phase Velocity

beats.narration_text: Wave Packet Spreading is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Free-particle dispersion ω=ħk²/2m: group velocity v_g = 2×phase velocity. Peaks march at v_p; envelope races at v_g.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Wave Packet Spreading. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: σ₀ (nm), k₀ (nm⁻¹), Time. Verify: Free-particle dispersion ω=ħk²/2m: group velocity v_g = 2×phase velocity. Peaks march at v_p; envelope races at v_g.. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Wave Packet Spreading. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: σ₀ (nm), k₀ (nm⁻¹), Time. Verify: Free-particle d

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from 00-wave-packet-spreading.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: wave packet spreading. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag σ₀ (nm) from 0.3 to 3. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of σ₀ (nm) from 0.3 to 3

beats.narration_text: Free-particle dispersion ω=ħk²/2m: group velocity v_g = 2×phase velocity. Peaks march at v_p; envelope races at v_g.. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of wave packet spreading. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Wave Packet Spreading simulation and add a second slider that controls σ₀ (nm). Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
